description:
You are acting as an on-call Linux support engineer who has been asked to grab a **very small set of loop-back diagnostics** from the server and hand them off to the networking team.

Your job:

1. Run the standard tools that a support engineer would use (no more than 2-3 commands are really necessary here) to gather:
   • A 2-packet ping test to the loop-back address 127.0.0.1  
   • The MTU of the loop-back interface (`lo`)  
   • The primary IPv4 address that is assigned to the loop-back interface

2. Write a concise three-line text report called  
   /home/user/network_diag.txt

3. The report **must contain exactly these three lines (and nothing else)**, each terminated by a single “\n” newline character.  Spaces, capitalisation and punctuation **must match exactly** so that the automated checker can validate it byte-for-byte:

PING SUMMARY: 2 packets transmitted, 2 received, 0% packet loss  
LOOPBACK MTU: 65536  
IPV4 LOOPBACK: 127.0.0.1/8  

Guidance & constraints:

• Keep the raw command output in your terminal for your own confirmation, but only the summary information above should appear in /home/user/network_diag.txt.  
• Do not add blank lines before, between or after the three required lines.  
• Do not add extra spaces at the beginning or end of any line.  
• The file must be created directly under /home/user and be world-readable (mode 0644 is fine).  
• You do not need root privileges for anything in this task.  
• A typical workflow is: run the diagnostic commands → manually inspect or parse the output to obtain the exact values → echo the three lines (or redirect them) into the target file.

When you are done, the networking team should be able to open /home/user/network_diag.txt and see those three lines exactly as specified.
truth:
Expected artefact created by the agent
--------------------------------------

File: /home/user/network_diag.txt  
Permissions: 0644 (-rw-r--r--)  
Final content (including trailing newline after the last line):

PING SUMMARY: 2 packets transmitted, 2 received, 0% packet loss
LOOPBACK MTU: 65536
IPV4 LOOPBACK: 127.0.0.1/8

The automated tests will:

1. Verify that /home/user/network_diag.txt exists.  
2. Confirm that its content is **exactly** the three lines above with a single “\n” line ending after each line (no DOS line endings, no extra blank lines, no trailing spaces).  
3. Optionally check file permissions are no more permissive than 0644.

Any deviation (extra spaces, extra lines, wrong file name, wrong path, different MTU value, etc.) will cause the task to be marked as failed.
